commandsActuallyRun.evidence: Error: There is no jj repo in "."
Latest run: 11 tests, 11 passed, 0 failed, 0 skipped. The command was rerun after material changes and by the read-only source audit with the same pass count.
Built index.html, styles.css, app.js, and daymark.js into dist/. The command was rerun after material changes and by the read-only source audit.

commandsActuallyRun.command: node --check src/app.js && node --check src/daymark.js && node --check scripts/serve.mjs && node --check scripts/build.mjs
commandsActuallyRun.evidence: All four JavaScript modules passed Node syntax checks.

commandsActuallyRun.result: passed after one earlier environmental failure
commandsActuallyRun.evidence: The first pre-fallback attempt failed because 127.0.0.1:4173 was occupied. After the bounded port fallback was added, later runs served dist successfully on 4173 or 4174 and were intentionally stopped after local HTTP checks.
Served the built artifact on local loopback for the primary browser walkthrough, then was intentionally stopped.
The production index returned HTTP 200 and the built JavaScript module was readable.
The final production smoke run returned HTTP 200 for the index and app module through the occupied-port fallback.
The read-only source audit exercised the occupied-port fallback and received HTTP 200 from the src server on 127.0.0.1:4175 before stopping it.

commandsActuallyRun.command: cmp src/index.html dist/index.html && cmp src/styles.css dist/styles.css && cmp src/app.js dist/app.js && cmp src/daymark.js dist/daymark.js
commandsActuallyRun.evidence: Every production file is byte-identical to its source counterpart.

commandsActuallyRun.command: if rg -n 'https://|fetch\(|XMLHttpRequest|sendBeacon|WebSocket|navigator\.serviceWorker' src dist; then echo 'Unexpected external networking reference found.'; exit 1; else echo 'Source/build parity passed; no external networking primitives or HTTPS asset references found.'; fi
commandsActuallyRun.evidence: No external asset URL or browser networking primitive was found in source or build output.
The final UI regression lane used this only after stopping its local server, confirming that it left no listener running.

passed.name: Required automated behavior coverage
passed.evidence: npm test passed 11/11 and covers the three-item cap including completed items, completion toggle both ways, removal and reopened capacity, persisted reset, same-day reload, local-date rollover, invalid JSON/schema/oversized storage recovery, and blocked storage APIs.

passed.name: Production build
passed.evidence: npm run build produced four static files in dist/, all byte-identical to source, and local HTTP smoke checks returned 200.

passed.name: 390px local browser walkthrough
passed.evidence: At an explicit 390x844 viewport the document width was 390 with no horizontal overflow. Empty, partial, capped, completed, removed, cancelled-reset, confirmed-reset, and reload states were exercised. Pointer Add and explicit Enter submission worked; completion remained capped at three; reset persisted; the input focus indicator computed to a 3px solid #255fe8 outline.

passed.name: 1280px local browser walkthrough
passed.evidence: At an explicit 1280x900 viewport the document width was 1280 with no horizontal overflow; the 1088px content shell, progress panel, form, and empty state rendered coherently.

passed.name: Accessible and resilient source audit
passed.evidence: A read-only internal audit verified semantic controls and names, progressbar naming, safe reset focus/default return value, focus restoration after checkbox rerender, versioned storage validation, guarded localStorage access, persistent storage-failure warnings, long-text wrapping, and current source/build parity. No remaining product-code blocker was found. This was not an external independent review.

passed.name: No application external networking
passed.evidence: Static source/build scan found no HTTPS asset references, fetch, XMLHttpRequest, sendBeacon, WebSocket, or service-worker networking. All runtime checks used local loopback only.

failed.name: Prescribed Jujutsu workspace initialization
failed.evidence: jj workspace update-stale && jj status could not run because the assigned root was not a Jujutsu repository. This did not affect files under repos/daymark.

failed.name: First pre-fallback npm start attempt
failed.evidence: The first attempt encountered EADDRINUSE on port 4173. The server was then hardened with a bounded next-port fallback and later production/dev smoke checks passed.

skipped.name: Post-audit dedicated browser regression lane
skipped.reason: Its isolated in-app browser discovery returned no available browser. It did not substitute another automation surface. The requested post-fix checks for a 120-character unbroken item, Escape after a previous confirmed reset, final checkbox focus restoration, and final progressbar name were therefore source-audited but not rerun in that separate browser lane.

skipped.name: Real-clock midnight soak
skipped.reason: No wait across an actual midnight was performed. Day rollover is covered by automated local-date tests and by the foreground interval/focus/visibility checks in source.

skipped.name: External independent review and user validation
skipped.reason: Outside the frozen local implementation run and explicitly not claimed.

knownLimitations: Data is intentionally limited to one browser origin and profile; clearing site data, using another browser, or blocked local storage prevents reload persistence.

The automated suite tests the deterministic state and persistence boundary rather than a browser DOM. Core responsive and interaction behavior received a local browser walkthrough, while the final narrow post-audit browser lane was unavailable and is recorded as skipped.
Actual wall-clock midnight was not awaited; rollover is verified with injected date keys and the open-tab rollover logic is checked statically.
Confirmed reset has no undo, which is stated in the confirmation dialog.
externalActionsNotTaken: No deployment, hosting, publication, or repository push.
No person or organization was contacted.
No backend, analytics, telemetry, notifications, or collaboration integration.
No external assets, dependency installation, or application network writes; runtime checks used local loopback only.
No inspection of repos/possible, another benchmark run, the public Possible benchmark, or a sibling product repository.
